options.name: progression_balancing
options.description:
A system that can move progression earlier, to try and prevent the player from getting stuck and bored early.

A lower setting means more getting stuck. A higher setting means less getting stuck.

options.name: accessibility
options.description:
Set rules for reachability of your items/locations.

**Full:** ensure everything can be reached and acquired.

**Minimal:** ensure what is needed to reach your goal can be acquired.

options.name: goal
options.description:
Determines the goal of the seed
Genie: Kill the required number of bosses followed by the Genie
Garlic Hunt: Find a certain number of Garlic Cloves

options.name: bosses_required
options.description: How many Bosses must be defeated in order to defeat the final boss

options.name: progressive_powerup
options.description: Whether powerups get unlocked in order or can be found directly as items.

options.name: get_previous_powerups
options.description: If powerups are not progressive, if you find jet, all previous powerups will be acquired as well (e.g. garlic, bull, dragon).

options.name: start_with_hj
options.description: High-Jump is active from the start. For people who like going fast.

options.name: remove_autoscrollers
options.description: Turn autoscrollers into normal scrolling stages.

options.name: boss_unlocks
options.description: Whether boss levels need to be unlocked by items found in the multiworld.

options.name: world_unlocks
options.description:
Whether overworld spots need to be unlocked by items found in the multiworld.
In a single world multiworld, this is forced to unlock Parsley Woods from the start.

options.name: number_of_garlic_cloves
options.description: How many Garlic Cloves are in the pool for Garlic Clove Hunt

options.name: percentage_of_garlic_cloves
options.description: What Percentage of Garlic Cloves are required to finish Garlic Clove Hunt

options.name: treasure_checks
options.description: Whether collecting the treasure in levels will grant a check

options.name: blocksanity
options.description:
Whether blocks with a face sprite count as checks
This does include hidden blocks and adds around 550 checks to the game
For more info check the Blocksanity Tips in:
https://github.com/randomcodegen/Archipelago_WL/blob/wl-dev/worlds/wl/docs/en_Wario_Land.md

options.name: trap_fill_percentage
options.description: Replace a percentage of junk items in the item pool with random traps

options.name: stun_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which briefly stuns Wario

options.name: timer_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of a receiving a trap which causes the timer to get set to 100

options.name: death_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of receiving a trap which causes the player to die

options.name: grease_trap_weight
options.description: Likelihood of receiving a greasy trap

options.name: music_shuffle
options.description: Whether level music is shuffled or not.

options.name: starting_life_count
options.description: How many extra lives to start the game with

options.name: death_link
options.description: When you die, everyone who enabled death link dies. Of course, the reverse is true too.

options.name: local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be in their native world.

options.name: non_local_items
options.description: Forces these items to be outside their native world.

options.name: start_inventory
options.description: Start with the specified amount of these items. Example: "Bomb: 1"

options.name: start_hints
options.description: Start with these item's locations prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: start_location_hints
options.description: Start with these locations and their item prefilled into the ``!hint`` command.

options.name: exclude_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an important item.

options.name: priority_locations
options.description: Prevent these locations from having an unimportant item.

options.name: item_links
options.description: Share part of your item pool with other players.

options.name: plando_items
options.description: Generic items plando.

options.name: start_inventory_from_pool
options.description:
Start with the specified amount of these items and don't place them in the world. Example: "Bomb: 1"

The game decides what the replacement items will be.
